title: Nova Lingua Predicate Expression
description: Structured AST for predicate expressions in Nova Lingua. Used by both refinements (function-record.signature.refinements[].expr) and property tests (function-record.properties[].expr). v0.1 covers five kinds — `var`, `lit`, `app`, `forall`, `exists` — sufficient to express the predicates that appear in standard-library function records (e.g. `length(output) == length(input)` for a refinement; `map(id, xs) == xs` for an identity property). Boolean connectives (and / or / not / implies / iff), comparisons (eq / neq / lt / le / gt / ge), and arithmetic (add / sub / mul / div / mod) are all expressed as `app` with the appropriate string `op`. Well-formedness (variable scoping, op arity, type compatibility) is enforced by the verifier engine, not by JSON Schema. v0.1 of function-record.schema.json does NOT yet require this AST form for `refinement.expr` or `property.expr` (they still accept the string surface form); switchover is planned for function-record schema v0.2.

predicate.description: Any predicate expression. Discriminated by the 'kind' field.

name.description: Name in scope. Three sources: (1) a quantified variable from an enclosing forall/exists, (2) an implicit function-record name like `input` or `output`, (3) a name bound by the verifier environment (e.g. a builtin function reference). The verifier resolves these.

var.description: Reference to a name in scope.

value.description: Literal JSON value. v0.1 allows bool, number, string, and null. For values that require fuller structure (lists, records, variants, function refs), use the value-expression sub-language as the literal payload — v0.2+ will integrate these formally.

lit.description: A literal value. The value sub-language (spec/value-expression.schema.json) is the structured alternative when the literal needs to be a compound value.

op.description:
Operator. Three forms accepted in v0.1: (a) a built-in name from the closed vocabulary below; (b) a content-address reference like `fn_<hex>` to a function in the commons; (c) a name in the enclosing scope (e.g. a higher-order argument). The verifier resolves the form.

Built-in v0.1 vocabulary (closed):
  - Boolean: and, or, not, implies, iff
  - Comparison: eq, neq, lt, le, gt, ge
  - Arithmetic: add, sub, mul, div, mod, neg
  - List: length, head, tail, nil, cons, map, filter, foldl, foldr
  - Identity/composition: id, compose
v0.2+ will expand and formalize this set.

args.description: Operator arguments in positional order. Arity-checking is the verifier's responsibility (JSON Schema cannot express it).

app.description: Function or operator application. All non-quantifier compound expressions go through this single form (principle 7: minimal orthogonal vocabulary). Comparisons, boolean connectives, and arithmetic are NOT separate kinds — they are `app` with the corresponding `op`.

vars.description: Variables introduced by this quantifier. Property tests instantiate them with random inputs.

body.description: Predicate that must hold for every assignment of the variables.

forall.description: Universal quantification — 'for all values of these variables, body holds.' Used heavily by property tests.

exists.description: Existential quantification — 'there exists an assignment of these variables for which body holds.' Less common than forall in properties; useful in refinements that assert reachability.
